message:
🌱 Add oras: Bad UX of `--oci-layout-path` flag working with `oras repo mission (#966)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "oras-1612-bad-ux-of-oci-layout-path-flag-working-with-oras-repo-tags",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "oras: Bad UX of `--oci-layout-path` flag working with `oras repo tags`",
+    "description": "Bad UX of `--oci-layout-path` flag working with `oras repo tags`. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current oras deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your oras version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras\nhelm list -n oras 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working oras installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review oras configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant oras configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras\nkubectl get configmap -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=oras\n```\n### What happened in your environment?\n\nI have an OCI layout that contains several tags, some are in format of FQDN."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Bad UX of `--oci-layout-path` flag working with `oras repo…",
+        "description": "This PR implements the new feature of showing repo tags with `--oci-layout-path` in `repo tags` command. This feature applies to the scenario where the user stores images from multiple repositories in the same OCI layout directory, and put the full references of the images as tags. For example, an\n```yaml\n$ oras repo tags --oci-layout ./repo\nexample.registry.com/foo:latest\ntest.com/bar:v1\nv2\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n oras -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oras\nkubectl get events -n oras --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Bad UX of `--oci-layout-path` flag working with `oras repo…\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "This PR implements the new feature of showing repo tags with `--oci-layout-path` in `repo tags` command. This feature applies to the scenario where the user stores images from multiple repositories in the same OCI layout directory, and put the full references of the images as tags.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "$ oras repo tags --oci-layout ./repo\nexample.registry.com/foo:latest\ntest.com/bar:v1\nv2",
+        "$ oras repo tags --oci-layout-path ./repo\nError: \"oras repo tags\" requires exactly 1 argument but got 0\nUsage: oras repo tags [flags] <name>\nPlease specify exactly 1 argument as the target repository to list tags from. Run \"oras repo tags -h\" for more options and examples",
+        "oras repo tags --oci-layout-path ./repo \"\"\nexample.registry.com/foo:latest\ntest.com/bar:v1\nv2"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [

+    ],

+    },
